arts.ttl: James Trafford is set to start for Manchester City against Wolves.
arts.sum: CityXtra has relayed a report from prominent X account, FPLMaineRoad, that James Trafford is set to start in goal for Manchester City in goal against Wolves in City’s opening Premier League fixture against Wolves. One of the most significant question marks surrounding Pep Guardiola’s team selection for Manchester City’s opening Premier League fixture of the season against Wolves is who would start in goal.

arts.ttl: How to watch Wolves vs Manchester City: TV channel and live stream for Premier League today
arts.sum: Guardiola begins quest to win back the Premier League with a new-look team

arts.ttl: Wolves vs. Man City: Preview, Prediction and Lineups
arts.sum: Rayan Aït-Nouri will line up against his former employers on the Premier League’s opening day.

arts.ttl: Guardiola vouches that 'desire is above everything' ahead of opening day visit to Wolves
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has shared his desire for Savinho to stay at Manchester City despite reports linking the Brazilian to Premier League rivals Tottenham Hotspur. City open their campaign on Saturday against Wolverhampton Wanderers at Molineux but Savinho, who joined the club from Girona last summer, will miss the fixture through injury, according to Guardiola.

arts.ttl: Savinho: Pep Guardiola wants Man City winger to stay for 'many years' but Brazilian ruled out of Wolves trip
arts.sum: Brazilian will miss first few games of campaign

arts.ttl: Pre-Match analysis: Wolves' opener against Man City
arts.sum: The directness of Wolves should be enough to exploit Man City's high line and cause problems similar to when these sides met last season. The two creative outlets in Fer Lopez and Jhon Arias will be able to utilise their pace and creativeness to cause problems for City's back-line, whilst Jorgen Strand Larsen will provide a focal point up front and can finish off chances when put into the box.

arts.ttl: Wolves learn three Manchester City stars are set to miss Premier League opener at Molineux
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has given an update on the injuries in the Man City squad in his pre-match press conference for season opener against Wolves. With the size and quality of City’s squad, absences aren’t felt like they would be for Wolves, but it’s never a bad thing to find out a couple of world-beaters won’t be travelling to Molineux.

arts.ttl: Four out, two in: Guardiola issues latest Man City injury update for Wolves opener
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola provides the latest update on the fitness of his squad, including Savinho, Ederson, Phil Foden, Josko Gvardiol and Rodri, ahead of Man City’s Premier League trip to Wolves on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola has provided an injury update on his squad ahead of Wolves clash
arts.sum: Manchester City take on Wolverhampton Wanderers tomorrow at Molineux. The match is City’s opening Premier League fixture of the 25/26 season. Ahead of tomorrow’s match, Pep Guardiola spoke to the media in his pre-game press conference. The Manchester City manager provided an update on the condition of his squad as he revealed that several players […] The post Pep Guardiola has provided an injury update on his squad ahead of Wolves clash appeared first on Esteemed Kompany.

arts.ttl: Vitor Pereira takes to social media with an ‘important’ Wolves message ahead of Man City
arts.sum: We’re fast approaching the first game of the Premier League season, with Wolves preparing to take on Manchester City on Saturday. Vitor Pereira says Wolves are ready to fight for every moment
